heading: Art. 140a. Case management; data collection and accessibility
label: 940a Art. 140a. Case management; data collection and accessibility

children.text: Collection and analysis of data concerning substantive offenses and procedural matters in a manner that facilitates case management and decision making within the military justice system, and that enhances the quality of periodic reviews under (article 146).section 946 of this title
Timely, efficient, and accurate production and distribution of records of trial within the military justice system.
Facilitation of public access to docket information, filings, and records, taking into consideration restrictions appropriate to judicial proceedings and military records.

children.heading: .—Protection of Certain Personally Identifiable Information
children.label: b .—Protection of Certain Personally Identifiable Information
children.text: Records of trial, docket information, filings, and other records made publicly accessible in accordance with the uniform standards and criteria for conduct established by the Secretary under subsection (a) shall restrict access to personally identifiable information of minors and victims of crime (including victims of sexual assault and domestic violence), as practicable to the extent such information is restricted in electronic filing systems of Federal and State courts.

children.heading: .—Inapplicability to Certain Dockets and Records
children.label: c .—Inapplicability to Certain Dockets and Records
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to provide public access to docket information, filings, or records that are classified, subject to a judicial protective order, or ordered sealed.

children.heading: .—Preservation of Court-Martial Records Without Regard to Outcome
children.label: d .—Preservation of Court-Martial Records Without Regard to Outcome
children.text: The standards and criteria prescribed by the Secretary of Defense under subsection (a) shall provide for the preservation of general and special court-martial records, without regard to the outcome of the proceeding concerned, for not fewer than 15 years.
